question: Is the beige 'Ordinary Oatmeal Box' in the center of the shelf replaced with the 'Breakfast Brands Blend' packaging?
Is the 'Breakfast Brands Blend' box rendered in a frontal view, correcting the angled perspective of the donor image?
Does the replaced cereal box match the height and width of the surrounding boxes on the shelf?
Is the price tag below the central box replaced with the new tag displaying '¥8.90' and '有机红富士苹果'?
Does the new price tag preserve the slight edge-curling deformation observed in the original tag?
Do the inserted box and price tag exhibit a greenish color cast consistent with the fluorescent lighting of the scene?
Are the specular reflections on the clear plastic price rail preserved (visible over the new box and tag)?
Is the bottom of the new cereal box correctly occluded by the transparent plastic price rail?
Are the text elements and logos on the 'Breakfast Brands Blend' box legible and recognizable?
Are the boundaries of the inserted elements seamlessly blended without harsh seams or halos?
Does the noise grain and sharpness of the inserted elements match the surrounding image details?
Are the neighboring cereal boxes and shelf structure preserved without distortion?
